revision.text:
{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族}} of {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Prince|ja|皇子}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|lt_ja=小碓命}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yorichika|ja|松平頼聰}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}} * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王}} (Sarureiō legend)

Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}} * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王}} (Sarureiō legend)

{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族}} of {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Prince|ja|皇子}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|lt_ja=小碓命}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yorichika|ja|松平頼聰}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}} * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王}} (Sarureiō legend)

{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族}} of {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Prince|ja|皇子}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}}.

This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yorichika|ja|松平頼聰}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}} * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王}} (Sarureiō legend)

revision.text:
{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|12=draft}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=draft}} of {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|12=draft}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|12=draft}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Prince|ja|皇子|12=draft}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|12=draft}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|12=draft}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|12=draft}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|lt_ja=小碓命|12=draft}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|12=draft}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|12=draft}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=draft}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yorichika|ja|松平頼聰|12=draft}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|12=draft}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|12=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|12=draft}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|12=draft}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|12=draft}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=draft}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=draft}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造|12=draft}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=draft}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|12=draft}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国|12=draft}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|12=draft}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町|12=draft}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|12=draft}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|12=draft}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|12=draft}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|12=draft}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|12=draft}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=draft}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=978-4642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|12=draft}} (Sarureiō legend)

== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|12=draft|12=draft}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yorichika|ja|松平頼聰|12=draft|12=draft}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|12=draft|12=draft}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|12=draft|12=draft}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|12=draft|12=draft}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=draft|12=draft}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造|12=draft|12=draft}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=draft|12=draft}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|12=draft|12=draft}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|12=draft|12=draft}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町|12=draft|12=draft}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|12=draft|12=draft}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|12=draft|12=draft}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|12=draft|12=draft}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=draft|12=draft}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sarureiō legend)
{{draft categories| }}

revision.text:
{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|12=draft|12=draft}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|12=draft|12=draft}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Prince|ja|皇子|12=draft|12=draft}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|12=draft|12=draft}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|12=draft|12=draft}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|12=draft|12=draft}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|lt_ja=小碓命|12=draft|12=draft}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|12=draft|12=draft}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yorichika|ja|松平頼聰|12=draft|12=draft}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|12=draft|12=draft}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|12=draft|12=draft}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|12=draft|12=draft}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=draft|12=draft}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造|12=draft|12=draft}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=draft|12=draft}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|12=draft|12=draft}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|12=draft|12=draft}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町|12=draft|12=draft}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|12=draft|12=draft}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|12=draft|12=draft}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|12=draft|12=draft}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=draft|12=draft}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sarureiō legend)

revision.text: {{統合文字|櫛}} '''神櫛皇子'''（かみくしのみこ（かみくしおう）/かむくし-/かんくし-/かんぐし-、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「神櫛皇子」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''神櫛王'''」、他文献では「'''神櫛別命'''」「神櫛命」「五十香彦命」とも表記される。『日本書紀』『古事記』とも事績の記載はない。 第12代[[景行天皇]]の[[皇子]]である。 == 系譜 == 『[[日本書紀]]』によれば、第12代[[景行天皇]]と、[[妃]]の[[五十河媛]]（いかわひめ）との間に生まれた[[皇子]]である。同書では、同母弟として[[稲背入彦皇子]]（いなせいりひこのみこ）の名を挙げる。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、景行天皇と、[[播磨稲日大郎姫|針間之伊那毘能大郎女]]（播磨稲日大郎姫）との間に生まれた皇子とする。また同母兄として、[[櫛角別王]]・[[大碓命]]・[[ヤマトタケル|小碓命]]（日本武尊）・[[倭根子命]]の名を記載する。 なお、『[[続日本後紀]]』[[承和 (日本)|承和]]3年（[[836年]]）3月19日条では、「神櫛命」の名で景行天皇第十皇子である旨が記されている。 == 墓 == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|神櫛王墓（[[香川県]][[高松市]]）}}]] [[陵墓|墓]]は、[[宮内庁]]により[[香川県]][[高松市]]牟礼町牟礼の'''神櫛王墓'''（かみくしのみこのはか、{{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}）に治定されている宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 402。。宮内庁上の形式は[[上円下方墳|上円下方]]。 この墓は、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に高松藩知事の[[松平頼聰]]によって再営された。毎年、王の命日と伝える[[10月20日]]に「正辰祭」と称する祭が斎行されている神櫛王墓説明板（高松市教育委員会設置）。。 == 後裔 == === 氏族 === 『古事記』では、神櫛王を木国之酒部阿比古・宇陀酒部らの祖とする。 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 右京皇別 [[讃岐氏|讃岐公]] - 大足彦忍代別天皇（景行天皇）皇子の五十香彦命（亦名を神櫛別命）の後。 * 右京皇別 酒部公 - 同皇子三世孫の[[足彦大兄王]]の後。 * 和泉国皇別 酒部公 - 讃岐公同祖。神櫛別命の後。 『[[続日本後紀]]』[[承和 (日本)|承和]]3年（[[836年]]）3月19日条によると、後裔を称する[[讃岐国]][[寒川郡 (香川県)|寒川郡]]の[[讃岐永直|讃岐公永直]]・[[讃岐永成|讃岐公永成]]らが[[朝臣]]姓を賜り、右京三条二坊に貫付されている{{Sfn|讃岐氏（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）8月17日条によると、同じく後裔を称する右京の[[讃岐高作|讃岐朝臣高作]]・[[讃岐時雄|讃岐朝臣時雄]]・[[讃岐時人|讃岐朝臣時人]]らが「[[和気氏|和気朝臣]]」姓を賜っている{{Sfn|別氏（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 === 国造 === 『日本書紀』では、神櫛皇子を[[讃岐国造]]の祖とする。 また『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」には、次の[[国造]]が後裔として記載されている。 * [[讃岐国造]] *: 軽島豊明朝（[[応神天皇]]）の御世に景行天皇の子の神櫛王の三世孫の[[須売保礼命]]を国造に定める、という。 *: のちの[[讃岐国]][[寒川郡 (香川県)|寒川郡]]・[[多度郡]]（[[香川県]][[さぬき市]]・[[善通寺市]]・[[多度津町]]一帯）に推定される『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』（八木書店、2013年）p. 269。。 == 信仰 == [[香川県]]に伝わる[[讃王|讃留霊王]]（さるれお、讃王）伝説によれば、景行天皇23年に讃留霊王は[[勅命]]を受け、讃岐入りして瀬戸内の悪魚退治を行い、同地に留まり[[仲哀天皇]]8年9月15日に125歳で[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]したという。この讃留霊王について、[[東讃]]では神櫛王のこととし（櫛梨神社社伝等）、[[西讃]]では[[武卵王]]（たけかいごのみこ：日本武尊の子）のこととしている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]] 監{{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「讃岐氏」|ref={{Harvid|讃岐氏（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「別氏（わけうじ）」|ref={{Harvid|別氏（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}。 == 関連項目 == * [[景行天皇]] * [[讃王]]（讃留霊王伝説） {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=神櫛王墓 |1-1=香川県の古墳 |1-2=高松市の歴史 |1-3=日本の陵墓 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみくしのみこ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:景行天皇の子女]] [[Category:香川県の歴史]] [[Category:讃岐氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[神櫛皇子]] to [[Kamukushi no Mikoto]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Prince|ja|皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|lt_ja=小碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yorichika|ja|松平頼聰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sarureiō legend)

[[ja:神櫛皇子]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E6%AB%9B%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:amukushi no Mikoto]] [[en:Kamukushi no Mikoto]] [[sn:Kamukushi no Mikoto]] [[zh:%E7%A5%9E%E6%AB%9B%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]]

revision.text:
{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|qq=}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=}} of {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Prince|ja|皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|qq=}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|qq=}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|lt_ja=小碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu City|qq=}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|qq=}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yorichika|ja|松平頼聰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Yorichika|qq=}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|qq=}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|qq=}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Seishiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kyuujihonki|qq=}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zukuri|qq=}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōunin Tennō|qq=}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki-koku|qq=}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsūji City|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadotsu Town|qq=}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūai Tennō|qq=}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}} * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} (Sarureiō legend)
{{translated page|ja|神櫛皇子|version=90241802|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Seishiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kyuujihonki|qq=}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zukuri|qq=}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōunin Tennō|qq=}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki-koku|qq=}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsūji City|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadotsu Town|qq=}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūai Tennō|qq=}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}} * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} (Sarureiō legend)

[[ja:神櫛皇子]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E6%AB%9B%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:amukushi no Mikoto]] [[en:Kamukushi no Mikoto]] [[sn:Kamukushi no Mikoto]] [[zh:%E7%A5%9E%E6%AB%9B%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Prehistoric Male Royalty]]
revision.text:
{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|qq=}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=}} of {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|qq=}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|qq=}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|lt_ja=小碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu City|qq=}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|qq=}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yorichika|ja|松平頼聰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Yorichika|qq=}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|qq=}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|qq=}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Seishiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kyuujihonki|qq=}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zukuri|qq=}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōunin Tennō|qq=}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki-koku|qq=}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsūji City|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadotsu Town|qq=}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūai Tennō|qq=}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}} * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} (Sarureiō legend)

This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yorichika|ja|松平頼聰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Yorichika|qq=}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|qq=}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|qq=}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Seishiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kyuujihonki|qq=}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zukuri|qq=}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōunin Tennō|qq=}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki-koku|qq=}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsūji City|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadotsu Town|qq=}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūai Tennō|qq=}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}} * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} (Sarureiō legend)
