revision.text:
{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|qq=}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=}} of {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|qq=}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|qq=}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|lt_ja=小碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu City|qq=}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|qq=}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yorichika|ja|松平頼聰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Yorichika|qq=}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|qq=}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|qq=}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Seishiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the {{ill|Sendai Kyuujihonki|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kyuujihonki|qq=}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni-zukuri|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zukuri|qq=}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Ōunin Tennō|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōunin Tennō|qq=}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki-koku|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki-koku|qq=}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki City|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Zentsūji City|ja|善通寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsūji City|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu Town|ja|多度津町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadotsu Town|qq=}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō Tennō|qq=}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Chūai Tennō|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūai Tennō|qq=}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}

In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=}} of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]].
== Genealogy == According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|qq=}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
On the other hand, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|qq=}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Kosu no Mikoto]] (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Furthermore, in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Zoku Nihon Kōki]] (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]])}}]] The tomb is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|qq=}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, [[:en:Matsudaira Yoritoshi|Matsudaira Yorichika]]. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|qq=}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|qq=}}.
In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kyuujihonki]] records the following national governors ([[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-zukuri]]) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōunin Tennō]], a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki-koku]] and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki City]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]], and [[:en:Tadotsu, Kagawa|Tadotsu Town]]).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=}}) as transmitted in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], in the 23rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai Tennō]].<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]] * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} (Sarureiō legend)
{{translated page|ja|神櫛皇子|version=90241802|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|qq=}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=}} of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=}} and other traditional records.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=}} of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]].
== Genealogy == According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|qq=}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
On the other hand, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|qq=}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Kosu no Mikoto]] (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Furthermore, in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Zoku Nihon Kōki]] (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]])}}]] The tomb is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|qq=}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, [[:en:Matsudaira Yoritoshi|Matsudaira Yorichika]]. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|qq=}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|qq=}}.
In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kyuujihonki]] records the following national governors ([[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-zukuri]]) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōunin Tennō]], a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki-koku]] and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki City]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]], and [[:en:Tadotsu, Kagawa|Tadotsu Town]]).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=}}) as transmitted in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], in the 23rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai Tennō]].<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]] * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} (Sarureiō legend)

In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kyuujihonki]] records the following national governors ([[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-zukuri]]) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōunin Tennō]], a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki-koku]] and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki City]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]], and [[:en:Tadotsu, Kagawa|Tadotsu Town]]).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=}}) as transmitted in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], in the 23rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai Tennō]].<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]] * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} (Sarureiō legend)

[[ja:神櫛皇子]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E6%AB%9B%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:amukushi no Mikoto]] [[en:Kamukushi no Mikoto]] [[sn:Kamukushi no Mikoto]] [[zh:%E7%A5%9E%E6%AB%9B%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:景行天皇の子女]]

revision.text:
{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|qq=}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=}} of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=}} and other traditional records.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=}} of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]].
== Genealogy == According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|qq=}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
On the other hand, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|qq=}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Kosu no Mikoto]] (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Furthermore, in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Zoku Nihon Kōki]] (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]])}}]] The tomb is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|qq=}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, [[:en:Matsudaira Yoritoshi|Matsudaira Yorichika]]. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|qq=}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|qq=}}.
In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kyuujihonki]] records the following national governors ([[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-zukuri]]) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōunin Tennō]], a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki-koku]] and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki City]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]], and [[:en:Tadotsu, Kagawa|Tadotsu Town]]).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=}}) as transmitted in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], in the 23rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai Tennō]].<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]] * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} (Sarureiō legend)
{{translated page|ja|神櫛皇子|version=90241802|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|qq=}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=}} of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=}} and other traditional records.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=}} of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]].

The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

Furthermore, in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Zoku Nihon Kōki]] (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]])}}]] The tomb is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|qq=}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, [[:en:Matsudaira Yoritoshi|Matsudaira Yorichika]]. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|qq=}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|qq=}}.
In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kyuujihonki]] records the following national governors ([[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-zukuri]]) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōunin Tennō]], a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki-koku]] and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki City]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]], and [[:en:Tadotsu, Kagawa|Tadotsu Town]]).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=}}) as transmitted in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], in the 23rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai Tennō]].<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]] * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} (Sarureiō legend)

[[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }}
[[ja:神櫛皇子]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E6%AB%9B%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:amukushi no Mikoto]] [[en:Kamukushi no Mikoto]] [[sn:Kamukushi no Mikoto]] [[zh:%E7%A5%9E%E6%AB%9B%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:景行天皇の子女]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:讃岐氏]]

{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|qq=}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=}} of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=}} and other traditional records.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=}} of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]].
== Genealogy == According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|qq=}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
On the other hand, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|qq=}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Kosu no Mikoto]] (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Furthermore, in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Zoku Nihon Kōki]] (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]])}}]] The tomb is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|qq=}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, [[:en:Matsudaira Yoritoshi|Matsudaira Yorichika]]. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|qq=}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|qq=}}.
In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kyuujihonki]] records the following national governors ([[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-zukuri]]) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōunin Tennō]], a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki-koku]] and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki City]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]], and [[:en:Tadotsu, Kagawa|Tadotsu Town]]).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=}}) as transmitted in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], in the 23rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai Tennō]].<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]] * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} (Sarureiō legend)

{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|qq=}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=}} of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=}} and other traditional records.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=}} of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]].
== Genealogy == According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|qq=}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
On the other hand, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|qq=}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Kosu no Mikoto]] (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Furthermore, in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Zoku Nihon Kōki]] (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]])}}]] The tomb is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|qq=}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, [[:en:Matsudaira Yoritoshi|Matsudaira Yorichika]]. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|qq=}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|qq=}}.
In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kyuujihonki]] records the following national governors ([[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-zukuri]]) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōunin Tennō]], a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki-koku]] and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki City]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]], and [[:en:Tadotsu, Kagawa|Tadotsu Town]]).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=}}) as transmitted in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], in the 23rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai Tennō]].<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]] * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} (Sarureiō legend)
{{translated page|ja|神櫛皇子|version=90241802|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }}
[[ja:神櫛皇子]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E6%AB%9B%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:amukushi no Mikoto]]
[[sn:Kamukushi no Mikoto]] [[zh:%E7%A5%9E%E6%AB%9B%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:景行天皇の子女]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:讃岐氏]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:神櫛皇子]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:神櫛皇子]]

{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|qq=}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=}} of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and other traditional records.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]].
== Genealogy == According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
On the other hand, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨稲日大郎姫}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子}}, [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Kosu no Mikoto]] (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Furthermore, in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Zoku Nihon Kōki]] (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]])}}]] The tomb is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, [[:en:Matsudaira Yoritoshi|Matsudaira Yorichika]]. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kyuujihonki]] records the following national governors ([[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-zukuri]]) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōunin Tennō]], a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki-koku]] and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki City]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]], and [[:en:Tadotsu, Kagawa|Tadotsu Town]]).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 讃王}}) as transmitted in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], in the 23rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai Tennō]].<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]] * {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} (Sarureiō legend)

{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|qq=}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=}} of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and other traditional records.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of the 12th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]].

The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
On the other hand, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨稲日大郎姫}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子}}, [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Kosu no Mikoto]] (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Furthermore, in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Zoku Nihon Kōki]] (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]])}}]] The tomb is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, [[:en:Matsudaira Yoritoshi|Matsudaira Yorichika]]. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|qq=}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.

Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|qq=}}” section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kyuujihonki]] records the following national governors ([[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni-zukuri]]) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|qq=}}. * During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōunin Tennō]], a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki-koku]] and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|qq=}} (encompassing present-day [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki City]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]], and [[:en:Tadotsu, Kagawa|Tadotsu Town]]).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|qq=}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 讃王}}) as transmitted in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], in the 23rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō Tennō]], Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai Tennō]].<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|qq=}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|qq=}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}).
