<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神櫛皇子]][[zh:神櫛皇子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神櫛皇子]][[zh:神櫛皇子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神櫛皇子]][[zh:神櫛皇子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神櫛皇子]][[zh:神櫛皇子]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0074|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Keikō|WD=Q329723}}|mother={{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ooiratsume|WD=Q11078587}}; {{ill|Ikawa-hime|WD=Q110450080}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672}}; {{ill|Prince Hikohitoooe|WD=Q11487893}}; {{ill|Prince Iokiirihiko|WD=Q11372557}}; {{ill|Prince Oousu|WD=Q11438297}}; {{ill|Prince Takekunikoriwake|WD=Q11545861}}; {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}}}
{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|1=unknown vital dates|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574588}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kiki|WD=Q842308}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ooiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11078587}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子|1=Prince Oousu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Kosu no Mikoto}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yoritoshi|en|Matsudaira Yoritoshi|WD=Q3387880|lt=Matsudaira Yorichika}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653200}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}” section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyuujihonki}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni-zukuri}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653200}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ōunin Tennō}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki-koku}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|1=Tado district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932605}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki City}}, {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsūji City}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu, Kagawa|en|Tadotsu, Kagawa|WD=Q1357346|lt=Tadotsu Town}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|1=sannou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632557}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 讃王|1=sannou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632557}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai Tennō}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|1=Tōsan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528343}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|1=Seisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629510}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神櫛皇子]][[zh:神櫛皇子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神櫛皇子]][[zh:神櫛皇子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神櫛皇子]][[zh:神櫛皇子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神櫛皇子]][[zh:神櫛皇子]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0074|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|father={{ill|Keikō|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}|mother={{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ooiratsume|WD=Q11078587|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume}}; {{ill|Ikawa-hime|WD=Q110450080|1=Ikawa-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}; {{ill|Prince Hikohitoooe|WD=Q11487893|1=Hiko-hito no Ō}}; {{ill|Prince Iokiirihiko|WD=Q11372557|1=Prince Iokiirihiko}}; {{ill|Prince Oousu|WD=Q11438297|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Takekunikoriwake|WD=Q11545861|1=Prince Takekunikoriwake}}; {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}}}
{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|1=unknown vital dates|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574588}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kiki (Shinto)|WD=Q842308}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11078587}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Joenkahofun|WD=Q11358496}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yoritoshi|en|Matsudaira Yoritoshi|WD=Q3387880|lt=Matsudaira Yorichika|1=Matsudaira Yoritoshi}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653200}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}” section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyuujihonki|1=Kujiki}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni-zukuri|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653200}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ōunin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki-koku|1=Sanuki Province}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|1=Tado district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932605}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki City|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsūji City|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu, Kagawa|en|Tadotsu, Kagawa|WD=Q1357346|lt=Tadotsu Town|1=Tadotsu, Kagawa}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|1=sannou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632557}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 讃王|1=sannou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632557}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai Tennō|1=Emperor Chūai}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|1=Tōsan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528343}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|1=Seisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629510}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}).

{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|1=unknown vital dates|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574588}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kiki (Shinto)|WD=Q842308}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11078587}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Joenkahofun|WD=Q11358496}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yoritoshi|en|Matsudaira Yoritoshi|WD=Q3387880|lt=Matsudaira Yorichika|1=Matsudaira Yoritoshi}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653200}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}” section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyuujihonki|1=Kujiki}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni-zukuri|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653200}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ōunin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki-koku|1=Sanuki Province}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|1=Tado district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932605}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki City|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsūji City|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu, Kagawa|en|Tadotsu, Kagawa|WD=Q1357346|lt=Tadotsu Town|1=Tadotsu, Kagawa}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|1=sannou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632557}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 讃王|1=sannou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632557}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai Tennō|1=Emperor Chūai}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|1=Tōsan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528343}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|1=Seisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629510}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神櫛皇子]][[zh:神櫛皇子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神櫛皇子]][[zh:神櫛皇子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神櫛皇子]][[zh:神櫛皇子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神櫛皇子]][[zh:神櫛皇子]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0074|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|father={{ill|Keikō|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}|mother={{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ooiratsume|WD=Q11078587|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume}}; {{ill|Ikawa-hime|WD=Q110450080|1=Ikawa-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}; {{ill|Prince Hikohitoooe|WD=Q11487893|1=Hiko-hito no Ō}}; {{ill|Prince Iokiirihiko|WD=Q11372557|1=Prince Iokiirihiko}}; {{ill|Prince Oousu|WD=Q11438297|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Takekunikoriwake|WD=Q11545861|1=Prince Takekunikoriwake}}; {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}}}
{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|1=unknown vital dates|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574588}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kiki (Shinto)|WD=Q842308}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11078587}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Joenkahofun|WD=Q11358496}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yoritoshi|en|Matsudaira Yoritoshi|WD=Q3387880|lt=Matsudaira Yorichika|1=Matsudaira Yoritoshi}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653200}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}” section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyuujihonki|1=Kujiki}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni-zukuri|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653200}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ōunin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki-koku|1=Sanuki Province}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|1=Tado district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932605}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki City|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsūji City|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu, Kagawa|en|Tadotsu, Kagawa|WD=Q1357346|lt=Tadotsu Town|1=Tadotsu, Kagawa}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|1=sannou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632557}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 讃王|1=sannou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632557}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai Tennō|1=Emperor Chūai}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|1=Tōsan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528343}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|1=Seisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629510}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}).

{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|1=unknown vital dates|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574588}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581068}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kiki (Shinto)|qid=Q842308}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11078587}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11438297}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|qid=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|qid=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|qid=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Joenkahofun|qid=Q11358496}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yoritoshi|en|Matsudaira Yoritoshi|qid=Q3387880|lt=Matsudaira Yorichika|1=Matsudaira Yoritoshi}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30929774}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21653200}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}}” section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyuujihonki|1=Kujiki}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|qid=Q2483673|lt=Kuni-zukuri|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21653200}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōunin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki-koku|1=Sanuki Province}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|1=Tado district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10932605}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|qid=Q857202|lt=Sanuki City|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|qid=Q191300|lt=Zentsūji City|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu, Kagawa|en|Tadotsu, Kagawa|qid=Q1357346|lt=Tadotsu Town|1=Tadotsu, Kagawa}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|1=sannou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632557}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 讃王|1=sannou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632557}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Chūai Tennō|1=Emperor Chūai}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|1=Tōsan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11528343}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|1=Seisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11629510}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}).
== References == {{Notes Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano (eds.), {{Cite book|language=ja|year=2010|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}|isbn=978-4642014588}}

{{Infobox person|birth_date=0074|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|father={{ill|Keikō|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}|mother={{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ooiratsume|qid=Q11078587|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume}}; {{ill|Ikawa-hime|qid=Q110450080|1=Ikawa-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}; {{ill|Prince Hikohitoooe|qid=Q11487893|1=Hiko-hito no Ō}}; {{ill|Prince Iokiirihiko|qid=Q11372557|1=Prince Iokiirihiko}}; {{ill|Prince Oousu|qid=Q11438297|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Takekunikoriwake|qid=Q11545861|1=Prince Takekunikoriwake}}; {{ill|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}}}
{{Unified Characters|櫛}}
'''Kamikushi no Mikoto''' (神櫛皇子) (【Year of birth and death unknown: {{ill|Year of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year of birth and death unknown|1=unknown vital dates|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574588}}】) is an ancient member of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581068}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kiki (Shinto)|qid=Q842308}} and other traditional records.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is referred to as “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, while in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} he is called “'''Kamikushi Ō'''”. In other texts, he is also denoted as “'''Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto'''”, “Kamikushi no Mikoto”, and “Itsukahana no Mikoto”. Neither the Nihon Shoki nor the Kojiki provide any account of his deeds.
He is a {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is the prince born of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and his consort, {{ill|Iikawa-hime|ja|五十河媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iikawa-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The same text also records his half-brother, {{ill|Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja|稲背入彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaseirihiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
On the other hand, the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} states that he was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja|針間之伊那毘能大郎女|lt=Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inahi Dairō-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11078587}}, and lists as his maternal siblings {{ill|Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja|櫛角別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushikaku Betsu-ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōusu no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11438297}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Kosu no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} (【also known as Japanese Hero】), and {{ill|Yawane no Mikoto|ja|倭根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawane no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Furthermore, in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|qid=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}} (Shōwa 3, 836, March 19 entry) it is stated under the name “Kamikushi no Mikoto” that he is the tenth son of Emperor Keikō.
== Grave == [[File:Tomb of Kamikushi-no-miko, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|qid=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}})}}]] The tomb is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} at the location of the '''Kamikushi no Mikoto Tomb''' (かみくしのみこのはか, {{Coord|34|20|35.24|N|134|7|17.26|E|region:JP-37|name=神櫛王墓}}) in Muramachi, Muramura, {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|qid=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The official form recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Jōen-kahō Kofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Joenkahofun|qid=Q11358496}}.
This tomb was reorganized in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era (1869) by the governor of the Takamatsu domain, {{ill|Matsudaira Yoritoshi|en|Matsudaira Yoritoshi|qid=Q3387880|lt=Matsudaira Yorichika|1=Matsudaira Yoritoshi}}. Every year, on October 20 – traditionally regarded as the anniversary of his death – a festival called the “Shōshin-sai” is observed.<ref name="説明板">Tomb explanation board (installed by the Takamatsu City Board of Education).</ref>
== Descendants == === Clan === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is regarded as the progenitor of clans such as {{ill|Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja|木国之酒部阿比古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuni no Sakube Abikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Uda no Sakube|ja|宇陀酒部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda no Sakube|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clans are recorded as his descendants: * Right Capital Imperial 【{{ill|Sanuki Clan|ja|讃岐氏|lt=Sanuki Clan|lt_ja=讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30929774}}】 – descended from Itsukahana no Mikoto (also known as Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto), the prince of Emperor Keikō. * Right Capital Imperial Sakube-kō – the descendants of {{ill|Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja|足彦大兄王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihiko Ōe no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third-generation descendant of the same prince. * {{ill|Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja|和泉国皇別 酒部公|lt=Izumi Clan Emperor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi no Ōe no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – of the same origin as the Sanuki Clan; also descended from Kamikushi Betsu no Mikoto.
=== Kunizo === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kamikushi no Mikoto is considered the progenitor of the {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21653200}}. Moreover, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}}” section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyuujihonki|1=Kujiki}} records the following national governors ({{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|qid=Q2483673|lt=Kuni-zukuri|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}) as his descendants: * {{ill|Sanuki Kunizukuri|ja|讃岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Kunizukuri|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21653200}}. * During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōunin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, a third-generation descendant of the prince, {{ill|Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja|須売保礼命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumeri Hore no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the national governor. * This is presumed to correspond to the later area of {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki-koku|1=Sanuki Province}} and {{ill|Samuke-gun|ja|多度郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuke-gun|1=Tado district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10932605}} (encompassing present-day {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|qid=Q857202|lt=Sanuki City|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|qid=Q191300|lt=Zentsūji City|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, and {{ill|Tadotsu, Kagawa|en|Tadotsu, Kagawa|qid=Q1357346|lt=Tadotsu Town|1=Tadotsu, Kagawa}}).<ref name="国造">『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 269.</ref>
== Faith == According to the legend of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} (also known as {{ill|Saruo|ja|讃王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruo|1=sannou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632557}} or {{ill|Sarureiō|ja|讃留霊王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarureiō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 讃王|1=sannou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632557}}) as transmitted in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, in the 23rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, Sarureiō received an imperial command, entered Sanuki, expelled evil fish from the Seto Inland Sea, and remained in the area, eventually passing away at the age of 125 on September 15 in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Chūai Tennō|1=Emperor Chūai}}.<ref>[[讃王|讃留霊王]] legend.</ref> In the eastern San region ({{ill|Tōsan|ja|東讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsan|1=Tōsan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11528343}}), this figure is identified with Kamikushi no Mikoto (according to the tradition of {{ill|Kushinushi Shrine|ja|櫛梨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.), whereas in the western San region ({{ill|Seisan|ja|西讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seisan|1=Seisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11629510}}) he is identified as {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}).
